metadata.description: Native-first, low-friction companion plugins for Claude Code.

plugins.name: task-queue
plugins.description: Resume bridge + auto-advance for Claude Code's native task list — re-surfaces a repo's unfinished tasks from earlier sessions, hydrates the queue from a committed roadmap/backlog file when present, and points the model at the next unblocked task as each one completes. The capture nudge weighs new work against the project's recorded direction (decisions/ADRs, roadmap) at capture time — alignment — and sequences low-blast-radius steps first; on a consequential prompt (destructive shell/DB ops, migrations, paid deps, deploy-to-prod) it raises a prompt-time review-gate asking the model to decompose and get per-task owner sign-off before queuing. Goes quiet once the policy is recorded in CLAUDE.md. Opt-in agent-mode fans independent tasks out to subagents. Open-decisions ledger (tq-ask.sh) + UserPromptSubmit re-injector + Notification alert ensure a question the model asks is never lost to queued prompts.

plugins.name: tidy
plugins.description: Tidy-as-you-touch: formats and lint-checks the file you just edited (fixing what's safe), flags it for decomposition when it grows past a size budget, and holds a subtractive posture (reuse before create, delete what a change makes redundant) so an active project trends down in complexity over time, scoped to the touched file. Over-budget files also surface automatically at session start; no manual trigger. Goes quiet once the standard is recorded in CLAUDE.md. /tidy:distill runs the full whole-project weight report on demand. Edit-time linting targets Go, web (eslint incl. jsx-a11y, stylelint), Python (ruff) and shell (shellcheck) — findings-only, the project's own tool, fast file-scoped tools only; surfaces manifest version pins on touch to flag outdated tech. Surfaces a touched file's blast-radius (Go via the exact `go list` importer graph, else git grep) for test coverage. Verification floor: runs the project's tests on Stop and blocks until green. /tidy:audit runs a proportional whole-project audit against the standards. Coverage ratchet nudges to characterize untested touched files before changing (opt-in strict gate).

plugins.name: charter
plugins.description: Know the project + own the owner relationship: gates substantive work on documented quality attributes (web projects get Lighthouse-aligned defaults), maintains a committed Claude-facing roadmap/backlog file, keeps a project map (file→responsibility index) so a session orients from the map instead of re-scanning the tree, and treats recorded decisions/ADRs as the alignment anchor (consult before reversing a choice) so past choices aren't re-litigated. Detects the project's ESTABLISHED conventions (component/UI library, styling, state, components dir, test framework) and surfaces them so new features REUSE what's there instead of introducing parallel patterns to clean up later — automatic until they're recorded, then quiet. /charter:align reconciles open/proposed work against the decisions + roadmap on demand (clean ≠ correct — surface drift and contradictions before the work is done). Generates the docs from the codebase/git history when missing; goes quiet once the policy is summarised in CLAUDE.md. Surfaces recently-merged commits for roadmap reconciliation. Records stack notes for modernization context. One compact, proportional brief (not a wall of per-doc prose). For non-technical owners it owns the owner loop (confirm intent in plain language → demo the result back → recap) plus a PreToolUse consent surfacing that reminds — never blocks — before consequential/irreversible actions (paid deps, destructive commands, DB drops, migrations), and nudges a thin plain-language owner doc layer (bus factor).

plugins.name: hud
plugins.description: A consolidated status line, scoped to the signals a status line is the best surface for: static health beacon, paused, agent-mode, the verification floor's ✓/✗ tests result, context-window fill %, git branch + dirty count, and model — read-only from existing plugin state, zero model-token cost, no idle timer. (Deliberately doesn't re-render the task list, docs-health, or last tidy action — Claude Code shows the tasks natively and charter nudges docs at session start.) Run /hud:setup once to wire it in (version-resilient).
